question_text: You would like to take a short cut in order to continue going straight ahead. What do you do?

answers.text: I pay particular attention to the road sign.
I proceed with caution.
I stop in order to turn or reverse and find another route.
I have an unconditional duty to give way when I leave the area.

explanation: The sign tells you that you are driving into an area with a pedestrian zone. As the sub-sign tells you that all driving is permitted, you can proceed cautiously.

question_text: You are driving at 40 km/h and need to continue straight out the road. What do you do?

answers.text: I will continue driving at the same speed, i.e. 40 km/h.
I pay particular attention to the pedestrian.
I pull over slightly to the left of the traffic lane.
I move my foot over to the brake.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the pedestrian who is walking on to the traffic lane between the cars. Therefore, you should pull over slightly to the left of the traffic lane, move your foot on to the brake and be ready to brake if the pedestrian carries on into the traffic lane.

question_text: You have reduced your speed in order to turn right here at the intersection. How will you continue?

answers.text: I make a right turn immediately.
I stop at the stop line.
I apply my brakes.
In the first instance, I drive out into the intersection a bit, stop at the cycle lane and wait until the lane is clear before turning.

explanation: Before a traffic signal, you should always pay particular attention to the traffic lights. Even though a red light is showing for the cyclist to your right, you must wait before turning until you are certain that the cyclist is stopping. Since the green light is showing, you don't need to stop at the stop line but drive into the intersection a bit and wait until the lane is clear before turning.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 50 km/h and need to continue going straight ahead at the intersection. You have caught up with the red car, which is braking. How will you continue?

answers.text: I will continue driving at the same speed, i.e. 50 km/h.
I overtake the red car immediately.
I pay particular attention to the white car that is stationary at the intersection.
I wait before overtaking and stay behind the red car for a while longer.

explanation: Due to the central reservation, no oncoming drivers are allowed in the two traffic lanes in your direction of travel, and the white car is stationary. Therefore, you may overtake immediately at the same speed.

question_text: You are driving at 30 to 40 km/h and need to continue straight out the road. There is no traffic behind you. How will you continue?

answers.text: I will continue driving at the same speed, 30 to 40 km/h.
I warn them with my horn.
I apply my brakes.
I pay particular attention to pedestrians.

explanation: The pedestrian is in the traffic lane, therefore, in this situation, you should pay particular attention to the pedestrian. However, as he is on his way out of your traffic lane, you shouldn't brake and hold back for him.

question_text: You are driving at 20-30 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.text: I overtake the cyclists immediately.
I warn them with my horn.
I wait until the cyclists have gone past the parked car before overtaking them.
I reduce my speed.

explanation: As the cyclists are apparently about to pull out due to the parked car and are not aware of you, you should reduce your speed and wait before overtaking.

question_text: You are driving at 30 km/h in a heavily built-up area. What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.text: I pay particular attention to the road sign.
I continue at 30 km/h after the road sign.
After the road sign, I increase my speed and continue at a maximum of 50 km/h.
There is an unconditional duty to give way at the intersection.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the road sign which indicates that an area with speed suppression measures is ending. You increase your speed after the sign and continue at a maximum speed of 50 km/h. The road sign indicates that you have an unconditional duty to give way.

question_text: You are driving into a heavily built-up area and need to continue straight out the road. How will you continue?

answers.text: I pay particular attention to the road sign.
After the sign the maximum speed I can drive at is 80 km/h.
After the sign the maximum speed I can drive at is 50 km/h.
I have an unconditional duty to give way when I leave the area.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the road sign that indicates that you are driving out a pedestrian zone. After the sign the maximum speed I can drive at is 50 km/h. You have an unconditional duty to give way when you leave the area.

question_text: You want to turn right as soon as possible. There are no drivers in the blind spots. How will you continue?

answers.text: I pay particular attention to the traffic lane lines.
I move to the traffic lane to my right.
I wait before moving to the traffic lane on the right, until the car behind has passed me.
I continue in my own traffic lane.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the traffic lane lines. The vehicle approaching from behind is a long distance away so you can move to the traffic lane to your right.

question_text: You are driving at 50 km/h and are about to overtake the lorry on your right. What do you do?

answers.text: I increase my speed and overtake the lorry.
I reduce my speed and abandon the idea of overtaking the car.
I should drive according to the rule on changing lanes where my traffic lane ends.
I should drive according to the rule on merging, where my traffic lane ends.

explanation: At the spot where the traffic lane lines end, the rule on merging applies. Therefore, you should reduce your speed slightly and wmerg in behind the lorry.

question_text: You are driving at 60 to 70 km/h and need to continue straight out the road. How will you proceed?

answers.text: I will continue driving at the same speed, 60 to 70 km/h.
I reduce my speed.
I am counting on the tractor holding back for me.
I apply my brakes.

explanation: Due to the oncoming tractor, which is indicating that it is turning to the left, you should pay particular attention to the types of manoeuvres made by the oncoming vehicle. You should therefore brake.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 60 km/h and want to enter the motorway to the left. There are no drivers in the blind spot behind you. How will you continue?

answers.text: I increase my speed.
I activate my left indicator.
I merge in to the motorway in front of the lorry.
I brake and wait before changing lanes.

explanation: As there is a lorry, which may only drive at a maximum of 80 km/h, behind you on the motorway, you mustn't continue in your own lane but increase your speed and merge in to the motorway in front of the lorry. Avoid crossing the broken white line at the driveway to the highway.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 70 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.text: I continue at the same speed, approximately 70 km/h.
I reduce my speed.
I pay particular attention to the drivers in front.
I pay particular attention to the road signs.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the road signs and not to the drivers in front.

question_text: You are driving at 60 to 70 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.text: I am particularly mindful of the course of the road.
A dangerous bend in the road.
I reduce my speed and continue carefully further along the road.
I increase my speed lightly.

explanation: Pay particular attention to the course of the road. The sign indicates several dangerous, difficult turns in the road, the first being on the right. I reduce my speed and continue carefully further along the road.

question_text: You should turn to the right here. There are no cyclists on the cycle path behind you. What do you do now?

answers.text: I turn to the right immediately.
I flash my headlights or give a warning with a short horn signal.
I wait for the oncoming cyclist to pass.
I am particularly aware that I am about to cross a double-lane cycle path.

explanation: The road sign indicates that you are about to pass a cycle path. The sub-sign indicates that you are about to cross a two-lane cycle path. You should pay particular attention to the oncoming cyclist and ensure that you are not a hazard or inconvenience when you turn. Right. Therefore, you must apply your brakes and wait until the cyclist has passed you before turning to the right. Therefore, you should not flash your headlights or give a warning with a short horn signal.

question_text: The oncoming lorry has just appeared. What do you do?

answers.text: I continue further along the road.
I pull over as far as possible to the right of the traffic lane.
I increase my speed.
I stop and wait for the oncoming traffic.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the lorry due to the large amount of space it needs. You should also be particularly aware of the limited width of the traffic lane. Drive as far to the right as possible and hold back for the oncoming driver.

question_text: You are driving at 80 km/h and need to continue straight out the road. How will you continue?

answers.text: I reduce my speed.
I pay particular attention to the traffic lane lines.
I am particularly mindful of the traffic further ahead.
I stop at the stop line.

explanation: You must always drive at a low speed over a railway track and pay particular attention to the traffic, for example, crossing trains, further ahead. You don't need to pay particular attention to the railway lines as you don't have to stop at the stop line.

question_text: You are driving at approximately 70 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.text: I will continue driving at the same speed, i.e. approximately 70 km/h.
I reduce my speed.
I am particularly mindful of riders.
I am particularly mindful of wild animals.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the road sign that warns you of the possibility of riders in the traffic lane. Pay particular attention to the use of the road. Reduce your speed, keep a good distance, and never use the horn signal.

question_text: You want to turn to the right here at the intersection. There are no cyclists or pedestrians behind you on the cycle path. How will you continue?

answers.text: I follow the cyclist who is turning.
I pay particular attention to the traffic lights.
I stop in front of the pedestrian crossing on the side street.

explanation: You are in the middle of the intersection and therefore do not need to pay particular attention to the traffic lights. The pedestrian is on the other side of the central reservation, therefore you must not stop in front of the pedestrian crossing.

question_text: You want to turn right at this intersection. There is a clear lane to the left and there are no cyclists coming from behind. How will you continue?

answers.text: I pay particular attention to the pedestrian.
I stop in front of the pedestrian crossing on the side street.
I increase my speed.

explanation: You must be particularly mindful of the pedestrian who is crossing the central reservation. You should hold back for the pedestrian.

question_text: You want to turn to the right at the intersection. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?

answers.text: I hold back at the Give Way sign if there is traffic crossing.
I turn to the right if the lane is free of traffic.
I unfortunately have to turn to the left at this intersection.
It is forbidden to turn to the left at the intersection.

explanation: The Give Way sign means that you have an unconditional duty to give way at the intersection. Even though you want to turn to the right as soon as possible, the road sign indicates that you must turn to the left here at the intersection.

question_text: You want to turn to the left at this intersection. What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.text: I drive forward to the centre of the intersection.
I pay particular attention to oncoming traffic.
I turn to the left immediately.
I hold back for the oncoming traffic.

explanation: Pay particular attention to oncoming traffic. You need to turn left and should therefore hold back for oncoming traffic.

question_text: The driver has left the grey car here. Which rules for stopping and parking apply here?

answers.text: May you leave the car here for up to 3 minutes?
May you park the car here?

explanation: As a rule, it is illegal to stop or park entirely or partially on the pavement within the urban zone signs. However, you are permitted to park on the pavement if this is indicated in marked parking bays or on special signs on the site. Outside the urban zone signs, vehicles with a permitted total weight of 3,500 kg are permitted to stop or park completely or partially in the outer verge or partially on the pavement.

question_text: When is the driver responsible for ensuring that passengers are wearing seatbelts?

answers.text: The driver is responsible for ensuring that all passengers under 15 years old are correctly fastened with a seatbelt.
The driver is only responsible for passengers in the front seat.
Lack of seatbelts increases the risk of accidents, as it may result in you being thrown out of the car.
While driving, the car's airbag poses a direct life threat if the seatbelt is not used.

explanation: The driver is responsible for ensuring that all passengers under 15 years old are correctly fastened with a seatbelt. This applies throughout the entire journey. So always remember to buckle up when you drive and make sure that all passengers do the same. It's about everyone's safety on the roads! Without the seatbelt, the airbag can deploy with a force that is directly life-threatening to passengers. The priority should always be to fasten the seatbelt to maximize protection in the event of an accident.

question_text: What should you pay particular attention to a bit further ahead?

answers.text: I am particularly mindful of the course of the road.
A dangerous bend in the road.
I should hold back for traffic from the side streets.
I am particularly aware that any crossing traffic has an unconditional duty to give way at the intersection further ahead.

explanation: You should pay particular attention to the sign "Dangerous intersection", which warns that there are risky conditions on the road ahead. The sign is erected on stretches where you do not expect a roundabout, pay particular attention to the course of the road. The sign indicates that the crossing traffic has an unconditional duty to give way.
